conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Does Alex normally live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. This is an important factor in determining eligibility.

Can you tell me who Alex currently lives with, and what your relationship or arrangement is regarding Alex's care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent, my ex-partner. But I do help out with their upkeep - I contribute towards things like clothes, school supplies, and other expenses they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining that. I have a few more questions to fully assess your situation.

Does your ex-partner currently claim Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, my ex-partner doesn't claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand the care arrangement a bit better.

Do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards Alex's upkeep each week? (For reference, Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I contribute about £26 a week towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one more important question:

Are you a UK resident, or otherwise eligible to claim UK benefits (for example, working in the UK or having certain immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Leeds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

- **Alex meets the age requirement** - at 8 years old, Alex is under 16
- **You meet the contribution requirement** - even though Alex doesn't live with you, you contribute £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week for an eldest or only child
- **You're a UK resident** - living in Leeds satisfies the residency requirement
- **No one else is claiming** - Alex's other parent is not claiming Child Benefit

Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim for a child who doesn't live with you as long as you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards their upkeep, which you do.

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for Alex. Keep records of your financial contributions as you may need to provide evidence of these payments.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant contributes at least £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week for an eldest or only child. According to the rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child who doesn't live with you if you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards their upkeep. The claimant is a UK resident (living in Leeds), satisfying the residency requirement. The other parent who Alex lives with is not claiming Child Benefit. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. While Alex lives with their other parent, the claimant contributes at least the equivalent of Child Benefit (£26 per week vs £25.60 required) towards Alex's upkeep, is a UK resident, and the other parent is not claiming. Under UK Child Benefit rules, a parent can claim even when the child doesn't live with them if they meet the contribution threshold.
